meaning: n.(被)限制,(被)禁闭,产期,分娩
examples: Last night he was being held in solitary confinement in Douglas jail.
Over-study hath done this, and somewhat too much of confinement.
It doesn't really mitigate against the confinement he has perpetrated on Lolita.
Three judges denied Mitnick bail and ordered him held in solitary confinement, where he stayed for eight months.
For now, any hopes of salvaging his NFL career are secondary to his impending confinement.
